docs.title: cli-template
docs.text:
Yet another template.

Design principles

  • DRY Don't Repeat Yourself
  • KISS Keep It Simple, Stupid
  • YAGNI You Ain't Gonna Need It
  • SOLID Single responsibility, Open/closed, Liskov substitution, Interface segregation, Dependency inversion

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
This project uses uv as a Python package and project manager.

uv sync --all-groups --frozen

This command will create a virtual environment and install all the project dependencies. You need to activate the virtual environment before continuing.

source .venv/bin/activate

Python & VS Code

  • If dependencies are not recognized in VS Code, you can run the command Ctrl+Shift+P > Python: Select Interpreter and select .venv.
  • After updating the dependencies, you can run the command Ctrl+Shift+P > Developer: Reload Window to refresh the development environment.

docs.title: App
docs.text:
# Run app
uv run main.py # Fail
uv run main.py --help

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
# Run CLI
cli-template # Fail
cli-template --help

# Install completion
cli-template --install-completion
source ~/.bashrc

# Use completion
cli [TAB] # ✨ cli-template
cli-template [TAB][TAB] # ✨ goodbye  hello

docs.title: Test
docs.text:
This project uses pytest for testing and pytest-cov for code coverage.

pytest -p no:cacheprovider

pytest cache

The -p no:cacheprovider option prevents pytest from using the cache, which makes the results more reliable. This is recommended for development but not mandatory, the CI will use it anyway.

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
# Build image
docker build -t cli-template .

Docker build

  • The -t option specifies a name and optionally a tag (format name:tag) for the image.
  • The . specifies the path or URL of the build context, in this case the current directory.
# Run container
docker run -it --rm cli-template

Docker run

  • The -it option keeps STDIN open even if not attached and allocates a pseudo-TTY.
  • The --rm option automatically removes the container and its associated anonymous volumes when it exits.

You can install and use completion inside the running container.

# Install completion
cli-template --install-completion
source ~/.bashrc

# Use completion
cli [TAB] # ✨ cli-template
cli-template [TAB][TAB] # ✨ goodbye  hello

docs.title: Docs
docs.text:
This project uses Material for MkDocs as a documentation framework.

# Serve docs
mkdocs serve

# Build docs
mkdocs build

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
Logs can be enabled by setting the LOGS_LEVEL environment variable. The possible values are DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR and CRITICAL. The default value is INFO.

LOGS_LEVEL=DEBUG cli-template hello # App
LOGS_LEVEL=DEBUG uv run main.py hello # CLI

docs.title: Lint / Format
docs.text:
This project uses Ruff as a Python linter and code formatter.

ruff check --no-cache # Lint
ruff format --no-cache # Format

Ruff cache

The --no-cache option prevents Ruff from using the cache, which makes the results more reliable. This is recommended for development but not mandatory, the CI will use it anyway.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• #4 🧑‍💻 Add completion
  • #3 📝 Add MkDocs
  • #2 🚀 Add Docker
  • #1 🎉 Probably last commit

docs.title: Release Engineering
docs.text:
This project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

Given a version number MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, increment the:

  1. MAJOR version when you make incompatible API changes
  2. MINOR version when you add functionality in a backward compatible manner
  3. PATCH version when you make backward compatible bug fixes

Additional labels for pre-release and build metadata are available as extensions to the MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH format.

docs.title: Release
docs.text:
From develop branch:

  • git checkout -b release/x.y.z, last checks, lint, format and tests
  • Update the references to the current version in the sources
  • uv sync --all-groups to update lock files
  • Update the CHANGELOG, set the new version with the release date
  • Commit as 🔖 Version x.y.z, create a release from the branch with a tag, and the content of the changelog
  • Merge branch into master and develop

docs.title: Hotfix
docs.text:
From master branch:

  • git checkout -b hotfix/x.y.z, last checks, lint, format and tests
  • Update the references to the current version in the sources
  • uv sync --all-groups to update lock files
  • Update the CHANGELOG, set the new version with the release date
  • Commit as 🔖 Version x.y.z, create a release from the branch with a tag, and the content of the changelog
  • Merge branch into master and develop
